title: Parameters cannot be used in RHS of input port (default value)
body:
Those snippets of code fail under the latest version of Icarus Verilog (both stable 12.0 and development `f5decd4`).

```sv
module Foo #(parameter F) (input logic i = F);
endmodule

module Test;
	Foo #(.F(1)) f();
endmodule
```

```sv
module Foo #(parameter F) (input logic i = F'(1));
endmodule

module Test;
	Foo #(.F(1)) f();
endmodule
```

Both result in:

```
$ iverilog -v -g 2012 test/sandbox.sv
[...]
Using language generation: IEEE1800-2012,no-specify,no-interconnect,xtypes,icarus-misc
PARSING INPUT
LOCATING TOP-LEVEL MODULES
   Test
 ... done, 0 seconds.
ELABORATING DESIGN
test/sandbox.sv:1: error: Unable to bind parameter `F' in `Test'
test/sandbox.sv:1: error: Failed to elaborate port default value.
2 error(s) during elaboration.
```

However, something like this works (which is not equivalent, it's just setting the initial value of a register, but syntactically it looks similar enough to make me expect consistent behavior):

```sv
module Foo #(parameter F) (output logic o = F);
endmodule

module Test;
	Foo #(.F(1)) f();
endmodule
```
code_blocks:
$ iverilog -v -g 2012 test/sandbox.sv
[...]
Using language generation: IEEE1800-2012,no-specify,no-interconnect,xtypes,icarus-misc
PARSING INPUT
LOCATING TOP-LEVEL MODULES
   Test
 ... done, 0 seconds.
ELABORATING DESIGN
test/sandbox.sv:1: error: Unable to bind parameter `F' in `Test'
test/sandbox.sv:1: error: Failed to elaborate port default value.
2 error(s) during elaboration.
